title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Guided Program Studio
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds import-safe deterministic build pipeline from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of import-safe deterministic build pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.
Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.
Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Main Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 1159 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-atomic-output-62v2cv
# Competency: ssg-atomic-output.
# Case 7972: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_atomic_output_62v2cv(stages: int = 10, completed: int = 10, import_safe: bool = True, staged_output: bool = True, prior_artifact_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_62v2cv = "62v2cv-vc2v26-7972"
    assert evidence_variant_62v2cv
    evidence_axes_62v2cv = (
        "reader62v2cv",
        "source7972",
        "parsevc2v26",
        "route62v2cv7972",
        "artifact797262v2cv",
        "recoveryvc2v267972",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_62v2cv)) == 6
    if stages < 8 or completed < 0:
        return False, completed, "pipeline-shape"
    if completed != stages:
        return False, completed, "partial-pipeline"
    if not import_safe or not staged_output:
        return False, completed, "effect-boundary"
    if not prior_artifact_kept:
        return False, completed, "destructive-output"
    assert completed == stages
    return True, completed, "atomic-pipeline"
